plan_sources: docs/RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md (Temporal RAG Program, lines 181-213; P2.1 gains, lines 164-171)

rows.collision_note: One name planned on FOUR carriers (document metadata spec:211, summary nodes spec:101, fact assertions spec:265, Qdrant payload spec:591). Must stay ONE enum (evergreen|slowly_evolving|versioned|event|ephemeral|unknown) fed by ONE classifier, or per-carrier drift becomes failure mode (a).
rows.evidence: grep -rn temporal_class backend/ = 0 hits; PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:67 confirms 'zero rows anywhere carry temporal_class'. T-HOOK-2 (CHECKLIST:195-198) targets the Ghost A summary contract. Nearest existing seam: backend/services/ghost_a.py:239-260 SummaryResult dataclass — latent_concepts sits at ghost_a.py:260 and is hydrated at ghost_a.py:649; temporal_class is specified to ride that exact seam. Backfill for existing rows = deterministic classifier (T-MAIN Phase 3), never paid regen.

code_symbols.role: wire-boundary mapper (T-HOOK-1, contract v3): worker returns time_expressions, Polymath persists temporal_captures + temporal_capture_version
canonical in-process dataclass fields (class ExtractionResult at ghost_b.py:2025)
re-hydration from Mongo rows on backfill/local paths

store_fields: Mongo ghost_b_extractions.temporal_capture_version (worker.py:2409 comment: extraction rows live in ghost_b_extractions)
rows.collision_note: FAILURE MODE (a): the same name time_expressions is ALSO planned as a NEW Ghost A summary-contract field (T-HOOK-2, CHECKLIST:195-198) — summary-level time expressions, a different seam from the built chunk-level extraction capture. When S1 builds it, either persist the summary field under a distinct name or document scope explicitly: chunk-level = temporal_captures (built), summary-level = T-HOOK-2 (unbuilt).
rows.evidence: runpod_flash_extraction.py:411-420 comment states the rename explicitly ('extraction rows persist them as temporal_captures + temporal_capture_version'). One concept, deliberate name drift at the wire boundary.

code_symbols.role: read-side probe: published_at is one of several accepted metadata spellings
alias coalescing into a source label's publication_date
Mongo read projection — field exists opportunistically on documents rows, never canonically written

store_fields: Mongo documents.published_at (opportunistic, source-dependent; NOT canonically populated — spec:70 shows ecommerce_pdf 1/79 has any date)
aliases: temporal_scope (CHECKLIST:166 only, undefined elsewhere — likely shorthand for valid_from/valid_to)
rows.collision_note: FAILURE MODE (b): publication time is planned under TWO names in the same checklist — P2.1 gains say 'published_at / temporal_scope' (CHECKLIST:166) while T-HOOK-3 and the spec say 'source_published_at' (CHECKLIST:202, spec:207,336). CHECKLIST:200 declares T-HOOK-3 merged with P2.1 as 'one implementation', so the duplicate-build risk is acknowledged, but the field NAME is not reconciled. Pick source_published_at (spec §5.1) and treat published_at as a read-side alias only.
rows.evidence: In code published_at is only ever READ as one of many alias spellings (orchestrator.py:2846,2993,3008,4731); no writer produces it canonically.

code_symbols.role: conflation site #1: any of five keys becomes document_date
Qdrant payload contract — declared, sparsely populated, not operational (spec:53)

rows.collision_note: FAILURE MODE (a) — the family's worst instance: ONE field carries THREE meanings (publication date, file-creation date, revision date) because docling_adapter.py:210-263 funnels all of them into document_date. This is exactly what T-HOOK-3 de-conflates. Spec:230 rules document_date remains a compatibility projection during migration, then deprecated — do NOT extend new features onto it.
rows.evidence: spec TEMPORAL_RAG_E2E_IMPLEMENTATION_REPORT_2026-07-12.md:52 names this conflation verbatim; population is near-zero anyway (spec:70 — 1 of 79 ecommerce_pdf docs).

aliases: published_at (CHECKLIST P2.1:166 spelling of the same concept)
rows.collision_note: See published_at row: same concept named twice across P2.1 and T-HOOK-3; one implementation is mandated (CHECKLIST:200), name must be settled as source_published_at.
rows.evidence: grep -rn source_published_at backend/ = 0 hits. Spec §5.1:207 defines it on the document temporal contract; spec:336 gives the mapping rule 'frontmatter published -> source_published_at'. Nearest existing seam: the docling metadata mapper docling_adapter.py:210-263 (currently writes document_date) + the doc_record write worker.py:2455-2460.

store_fields: PLANNED Mongo collection: source_episodes (spec:611), key episode_id (spec:238), index (corpus_id, temporal_class, source_published_at) on sibling source_versions (spec:621)
rows.collision_note: No code collision — 'episode' is unused in backend/. Design lineage is Graphiti's episode concept (spec:147-153).
rows.evidence: grep -rn episode backend/services backend/models backend/scripts = 0 relevant hits. Nearest existing seams: document versioning fields ChunkMetadata.supersedes/superseded_by/is_latest (backend/models/contracts.py:112-115) and immutable per-chunk evidence rows in Mongo ghost_b_extractions (worker.py:2409) — episodes generalize these into an immutable evidence unit.

store_fields: PLANNED Mongo collection: fact_assertions (spec:612), unique(assertion_id) sha256(source_version+span+triple+scope+validity) (spec:255,624), fields valid_from/valid_to/recorded_at/superseded_at/assertion_modality/temporal_class
PLANNED Qdrant points chunk_type='temporal_assertion' with pointer to source chunk (spec:516)
rows.collision_note: Two collisions. (a1) fact_type='timestamp' EXISTS in Ghost B FACT_TYPES (backend/services/ghost_b.py:134-137) and is the tempting-but-forbidden carrier — spec:347 explicitly rules 'Do not overload fact_type=timestamp'; assertion validity is a separate envelope. (a2) superseded_at ALREADY exists with a different meaning: job-lease supersession (backend/services/ingestion/job_leases.py:435, job_control.py:60,113) and chunk superseded_by (contracts.py:115) — three lifecycles will share one word once fact_assertions.superseded_at lands; keep them namespaced by collection. Also note 'assertion' pervasively means test-assertion in scripts (e.g. backend/scripts/p0_1_summary_integrity.py:28) — grep noise, not a real conflict.
rows.evidence: grep for fact_assertions/assertion_id in backend/ = 0 hits. Nearest existing seams: ExtractedFact/facts on ExtractionResult (contracts.py:90-93, ghost_b.py FACT_TYPES) and Neo4j RELATES_TO edges whose timeless-collapse (spec:59, MERGE aggregates all evidence onto one edge) is the defect assertions fix; versioned RELATES_TO rides the P2.5 edge migration (critique S12, one migration).

store_fields: PLANNED: shared Qdrant filter over assertion/payload temporal fields (spec:509-546); the fields it would filter exist as declared-but-dead contract fields RetrievalPayload.document_date/valid_from/valid_to (backend/models/contracts.py:151-153)
rows.canonical: temporal eligibility service (ONE service across Tier-0/Fast/Hybrid/Graph)
rows.collision_note: FAILURE MODE (a): do NOT confuse with the BUILT temporal_support/has_temporal machinery in the insight orchestrator (_has_temporal_source_support backend/services/graph/orchestrator.py:3003-3014, flags at :605,:1265,:5726) — that answers 'does this evidence row carry ANY date metadata' for packet curation/prompt hints; it is not validity filtering and must not be reused as the eligibility predicate. Second nearby-but-distinct seam: query_plan.py ConstraintKind 'temporal' (backend/services/retriever/query_plan.py:39, temporal_day_0 at :362-370) is lexical query-constraint tagging, not eligibility.
rows.evidence: CHECKLIST:209 'ONE eligibility service across Tier-0/Fast/Hybrid/Graph'; spec:25,441-450 (LLM never generates the predicate; code compiles TemporalIntent into storage filters). No code implements it; contracts.py:151-153 valid_from/valid_to are the dormant fields it needs (spec:53 'the shape exists ... not operational').

rows.canonical: T-HOOK-3 date de-conflation (merged with P2.1 bibliographic capture — one implementation)
rows.collision_note: FAILURE MODE (b) — pre-empted but watch it: T-HOOK-3 and the P2.1 'deterministic bibliographic capture/backfill' describe the same work; CHECKLIST:200 and critique S2 both pin them to ONE implementation. Residual risk is only the field-name split (published_at vs source_published_at, see those rows).
rows.evidence: CHECKLIST:200-202; PLAN_CRITIQUE S2 (row 81): 'T-HOOK-3 = P2.1 bibliographic item', deterministic doc-level backfill (498 v2 documents-only rows, no parent rewrites, inside the freeze). No de-conflated fields exist yet in code.

store_fields: PLANNED: per-corpus capability readiness value temporal_unavailable|partial|strict_ready riding the corpus readiness record
rows.collision_note: Lands on the SAME seam as the S6 readiness split (operational vs metadata-quality vs temporal capability); the enum must live beside — not inside — fully_enriched, because fully_enriched is a pipeline-stage label (backend/services/ingestion/readiness.py:112 FULLY_ENRICHED_STAGES) that must stop implying librarian-grade or temporal-grade metadata (CHECKLIST:169-171).
rows.evidence: grep -rn temporal_unavailable backend/ = 0 hits. Nearest existing seam: backend/services/ingestion/readiness.py — build_corpus_readiness_record (:309), build_corpus_readiness_snapshot (:330), compute_corpus_readiness (:1105); CHECKLIST:210 says 'same seam as the operational-vs-metadata-quality readiness split adopted in Audit Delta 2'.
